candidates.notes: AR baseline comparison completed. AR dominates DLM in both accuracy (96% vs 71.2% GSM8K) and speed (71 vs 31 TPS at batch=1, 471 vs 31 TPS at batch=8). Cross-tokenizer speculative decoding (UAD) is slower than greedy (48 vs 71 TPS). Speculative batch=8 unsupported in HF transformers. The paper should frame DLM acceleration as narrowing the DLM-AR gap rather than competing with AR speed. 3 of 4 configs produced valid measurements.

limitations: Speculative decoding batch=8 not supported by HF transformers assisted_generation (vLLM could not be installed due to PyTorch nightly incompatibility)
Cross-tokenizer UAD overhead makes speculative decoding slower than greedy; a same-tokenizer draft model (Qwen2.5-0.5B-Instruct) might yield better results but was not available
Comparison uses different model families (Qwen2.5-7B vs LLaDA-8B) which differ in architecture, training data, and instruct tuning
